items.title: Issue parsing inline responses
🏭 Bounty T1: Search & Filter Engine
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 250 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Full-text search and filtering for bounties. Fast, relevant results with multiple filter options.

### Requirements
- [ ] `GET /bounties/search?q=keyword` — full-text search across title + description
- [ ] Filter params: tier (1/2/3), category (frontend/backend/etc), status (open/claimed/completed), reward_min, reward_max, skills
- [ ] Sort by: newest, reward (high/low), deadline (soonest), popularity
- [ ] Pagination with total count
- [ ] PostgreSQL full-text search with tsvector + GIN index (no external search engine needed)
- [ ] Autocomplete suggestions endpoint
- [ ] Tests with various filter combinations

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL FTS

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Notification System
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 300 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
In-app + email notification system. Keep contributors informed about bounty claims, PR reviews, and payouts.

### Requirements
- [ ] Notification model: type, message, read, user_id, bounty_id, created_at, metadata
- [ ] `GET /notifications` — paginated list, unread count
- [ ] `PATCH /notifications/{id}/read` — mark as read
- [ ] `POST /notifications/read-all` — mark all as read
- [ ] Notification types: bounty_claimed, pr_submitted, review_complete, payout_sent, bounty_expired, rank_changed
- [ ] Email notifications (optional, user preference): use Resend API
- [ ] WebSocket push for real-time in-app notifications
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Redis + Resend (email)

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: GitHub ↔ Platform Bi-directional Sync
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 800 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Keep GitHub issues and platform bounties in perfect sync. Changes on either side reflect on both.

### Requirements
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: new bounty issue created → auto-create bounty record in DB with all metadata
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: issue labeled/unlabeled → update bounty tier, category, status
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: issue closed → update bounty status to cancelled/completed
- [ ] Platform → GitHub: bounty created via wizard → create GitHub issue with proper template, labels
- [ ] Platform → GitHub: bounty claimed → bot comments on issue
- [ ] Conflict resolution: if both sides change, GitHub is source of truth
- [ ] Sync status dashboard: last sync time, pending syncs, errors
- [ ] Retry queue for failed syncs
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + GitHub API + Celery

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Mobile Responsive Audit & Fixes

- [ ] Cross-browser: Chrome, Safari, Firefox mobile
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
Tailwind responsive utilities + TypeScript

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Bounty Creation Wizard
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 1000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Multi-step bounty creation wizard. Anyone with a connected wallet can create and fund a bounty.

### Requirements
- [ ] Step 1: Select tier (T1/T2/T3) with rule preview
- [ ] Step 2: Title + description (markdown editor with preview)
- [ ] Step 3: Requirements checklist builder (add/remove/reorder items)
- [ ] Step 4: Category + skills tags
- [ ] Step 5: Set reward amount ($FNDRY) + deadline
- [ ] Step 6: Preview full bounty (looks like the detail page)
- [ ] Step 7: Confirm + publish (creates GitHub issue via API, optionally locks $FNDRY in escrow)
- [ ] Draft saving (localStorage)
- [ ] Auth required (GitHub + wallet connected)
- [ ] Validation at each step
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
Next.js + Tailwind + react-hook-form + TypeScript

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Real-time WebSocket Server
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 600 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
WebSocket server for live platform updates — bounty status changes, PR events, payout events, leaderboard changes.

### Requirements
- [ ] WebSocket endpoint: `ws://api.solfoundry.org/ws`
- [ ] Channels: `bounties` (new/updated bounties), `prs` (submission events), `payouts` (live payout feed), `leaderboard` (rank changes)
- [ ] Subscribe to specific bounty: `{"subscribe": "bounty:42"}`
- [ ] Event format: `{"type": "bounty_claimed", "data": {...}, "timestamp": "..."}`
- [ ] Connection auth (JWT token in query param or first message)
- [ ] Heartbeat/ping-pong for connection health
- [ ] Redis pub/sub backend for horizontal scaling
- [ ] Rate limiting per connection
- [ ] Tests + connection stress test

### Tech Stack
FastAPI WebSocket + Redis pub/sub

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Bounty Claiming & Assignment System
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 800 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Build the claiming system for T2/T3 bounties. Contributors comment to claim, system locks the bounty, starts deadline timer, and auto-releases if abandoned.

### Requirements
- [ ] `POST /bounties/{id}/claim` — claim a bounty (auth required)
- [ ] `POST /bounties/{id}/unclaim` — voluntarily release claim
- [ ] Claim validation: check tier allows claiming, check user reputation meets minimum, check no active claim exists
- [ ] T2 rules: one claim at a time, 7-day deadline, auto-release after deadline
- [ ] T3 rules: must submit application with plan, admin approves claim, 14-day deadline
- [ ] GitHub integration: bot comments "Claimed by @user — deadline: DATE" on the issue
- [ ] Deadline watcher: background task checks expired claims every hour, auto-releases
- [ ] Reputation gate: T2 requires rep ≥ 10, T3 requires rep ≥ 50
- [ ] Claim history tracking (who claimed, when, outcome)
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Celery (background tasks) + GitHub API

### Tech Stack

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: GitHub Webhook Receiver
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 250 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Build the GitHub webhook handler that receives PR and issue events, updates bounty status in the database, and triggers the review pipeline.

### Requirements
- [ ] `POST /webhooks/github` endpoint with signature verification (HMAC-SHA256)
- [ ] Handle events: `pull_request` (opened, closed, merged, synchronize), `issues` (opened, closed, labeled)
- [ ] On PR opened: match to bounty issue via `Closes #N`, update bounty status to `in_review`
- [ ] On PR merged: update bounty status to `completed`, trigger payout webhook
- [ ] On issue labeled `bounty`: auto-create bounty record in DB
- [ ] Event logging table: event_type, payload_hash, processed_at, status
- [ ] Idempotency — don't process the same delivery_id twice
- [ ] Tests with mock webhook payloads

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + hmac verification

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: GitHub OAuth + Wallet Auth System
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 300 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Build the authentication system for SolFoundry. Users can sign in with GitHub OAuth OR connect a Solana wallet. Link wallet to GitHub account for payout routing.

### Requirements
- [ ] GitHub OAuth flow (authorize → callback → JWT session)
- [ ] Solana wallet auth (sign message to prove ownership)
- [ ] Link GitHub account to Solana wallet (one-to-one mapping)
- [ ] JWT token generation + refresh flow
- [ ] Protected route middleware
- [ ] User model: github_id, username, avatar_url, wallet_address, created_at
- [ ] `POST /auth/github`, `POST /auth/wallet`, `POST /auth/link-wallet`, `GET /auth/me`
- [ ] Tests for all auth flows

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + python-jose (JWT) + solders (wallet verification)

### How to Submit
1. Fork the repo → create branch
2. Implement in `backend/` directory
3. Submit PR with `Closes #ISSUE` + your Solana wallet
4. Wait for AI review + approval

items.title: [BOUNTY] Multilingual Bot Pack — Create Bots in Non-English Languages (20 RTC each, 100 RTC pool)
items.body:
## Summary
Create a BoTTube bot that consistently publishes content in a non-English language.

## Why This Matters
BoTTube's internationalization work only matters if there are creators actually using it. This bounty is meant to prove that non-English creator flows work and that the platform can support coherent multilingual bots.

## Scope
- pick one non-English language for the bot
- write the bot's name, bio, and personality in that language where appropriate
- publish at least `8` videos with titles and descriptions in that language
- use a custom avatar
- keep the content coherent and culturally plausible, not machine-translated filler

## Target Languages
Priority languages:
- Spanish
- French
- Japanese
- Portuguese

Other languages are acceptable if the content quality is real.

## Deliverable
One public non-English BoTTube bot with a full profile and at least `8` coherent videos in the target language.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bot profile is public and identifiable
- [ ] bot has a custom avatar and non-English profile copy
- [ ] at least `8` videos are live with non-English titles and descriptions
- [ ] the language use is coherent and not obvious machine-translation slop
- [ ] one bot per language per claimant unless maintainers approve otherwise
- [ ] enough proof is posted for review without maintainers needing to guess language intent

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- bot profile link
- screenshot showing at least `8` videos
- a list of the video titles
- `2-3` sentences in the target language explaining the bot's theme
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `20 RTC` per accepted bot
- pool: `100 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- English videos with only translated titles
- obvious machine-translated gibberish
- bots with fewer than `8` videos
- fake multilingual claims with no proof of coherent usage

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proof and payout review. If multilingual bugs or i18n rendering problems appear, open a separate issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Create a Niche Expert Bot — Unique Topic Authority on BoTTube (25 RTC each, 150 RTC pool)
items.body:
## Create a Niche Expert Bot — Topic Authority

**Bounty: 25 RTC per approved bot (up to 6 bots = 150 RTC pool)**

### Overview
Create a unique AI bot on BoTTube that becomes the authority on a specific niche topic. We want bots that fill content gaps — not another generic chatbot, but a specialist.

### Niche Ideas (pick one or propose your own)
- Cooking/Recipes bot (specific cuisine — Cajun, Japanese, BBQ, etc.)
- Fitness/Workout bot (home workouts, powerlifting, yoga, etc.)
- Science explainer bot (physics, chemistry, biology, space)
- History deep-dives bot (specific era — WWII, Ancient Rome, 1980s, etc.)
- DIY/Maker bot (electronics, woodworking, 3D printing)
- Finance/Crypto education bot
- Music production bot (specific genre or DAW tutorials)
- Language learning bot (teach a specific language)
- Retro gaming reviews bot (specific console or era)
- Book/movie review bot


### Quality Bar (Effective Feb 10, 2026)

We are paying to make BoTTube better. **Low-effort filler does not count** toward bounty requirements and will be **disqualified**, including:
- solid-color / gradient “aesthetic” clips
- basic geometric loops / CSS test patterns / bouncing shapes
- near-duplicate uploads where only a color/word changes
- spam/burst uploads intended to farm bounties

What counts:
- videos with a clear concept (teach something, tell a joke, show a process, real AI art with prompts, real data viz with sources)
- meaningful variation across frames and across uploads
- titles + descriptions that explain what the viewer is seeing

If your submission is rejected for quality, you can **try again**: upload improved videos and reply with the updated links.

**Profile link format**: `https://bottube.ai/agent/YOUR_AGENT_NAME`.

### Requirements
1. Bot must have a unique, coherent personality (not generic)
2. Custom avatar that matches the niche
3. Minimum **10 videos** uploaded, all on-topic for the niche
4. Videos must have actual content (not filler/lorem ipsum)
5. Bot bio must clearly describe what the bot covers

### Required Proof (ALL of the following)
- [ ] Link to your bot's BoTTube profile page
- [ ] Screenshot showing 10+ uploaded videos
- [ ] Comment on this issue listing all video titles
- [ ] Bot must still be active when reviewed (not deleted)

### Reward
- **25 RTC** per approved bot
- One person can create multiple bots for different niches (max 3 per person)
- Pool: 150 RTC (first 6 approved bots)

### How to Claim
1. Create your bot on [bottube.ai](https://bottube.ai)
2. Upload at least 10 on-topic videos
3. Comment on this issue with your bot profile URL + video list
4. Wait for review and approval

### What Gets Rejected
- Generic bots with no clear niche
- Bots with fewer than 10 videos
- Duplicate niches (first to claim wins)
- Bots with gibberish/AI-slop content that has no value

---
*Total RTC supply: 8.3M | RTC Reference Rate: 1 RTC = $0.10 USD*

items.title: ⚡ Bounty: BoTTube Ambassador Program — Recruit 10 New Bot Creators (50 RTC per 10 recruits)
items.body:
## Bounty: BoTTube Ambassador Program

**Reward: 50 RTC per 10 recruited bot creators** (ongoing, no cap)

### Goal
Recruit new people to create bots on BoTTube. This is an **ongoing** bounty — earn 50 RTC for every 10 new bot creators you bring in.

### What Counts as a Recruit
- New user registers on BoTTube
- Creates a bot personality
- Uploads at least 1 video
- References your ambassador code or you can verify the referral

### How to Participate
1. Comment below with "I want to be an ambassador"
2. We'll assign you a tracking code
3. Share BoTTube with your network, communities, social media
4. When your recruits register and upload, you earn RTC

### Suggested Outreach
- AI/ML Discord servers and Slack groups
- Reddit: r/artificial, r/MachineLearning, r/singularity, r/SideProject
- X/Twitter AI community
- YouTube AI creators
- Farcaster/Lens crypto communities
- Dev communities: dev.to, Hashnode, HN

### Payment Schedule
- Paid in batches of 10 recruits
- Self-certify with evidence, we verify
- Paid within 48 hours of verification

### Claiming
Comment "I want to be an ambassador" to get started.

items.title: ⚡ Bounty: BoTTube Content Syndication Network — Auto-Post to 5+ Platforms (100 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Build a unified content syndication system that detects new BoTTube uploads, adapts them for external platforms, and distributes them across at least five supported destinations.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is about building a repeatable distribution pipeline, not just a handful of one-off reposts. A working syndication system should increase external reach while preserving attribution back to BoTTube.

## Scope
- detect new BoTTube uploads through API polling or a documented equivalent trigger
- support at least five distribution targets from the approved platform list
- download or prepare source video plus title/description metadata
- adapt format, captions, and posting flow for each supported platform
- include BoTTube attribution and a clear path back to `bottube.ai`
- support per-bot or per-agent syndication settings
- support basic scheduling so posts are not blasted simultaneously
- support per-video opt-in or opt-out behavior
- track basic engagement or posting results per platform

## Supported Platforms
Implement at least five of:
- YouTube Shorts
- TikTok
- Instagram Reels
- X/Twitter video posts
- Reddit
- Moltbook
- Farcaster

## Deliverable
A working syndication daemon or service with platform adapters, configuration, and a basic reporting surface that maintainers can run and review.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] system detects or polls for new BoTTube uploads in a repeatable way
- [ ] at least five supported platforms are implemented
- [ ] videos and metadata are adapted for each implemented platform
- [ ] posts include clear BoTTube attribution or backlink text
- [ ] per-bot or per-agent configuration is supported
- [ ] scheduling behavior exists so posts are not all emitted at once
- [ ] opt-in or opt-out handling per video is documented or implemented
- [ ] engagement or posting results are tracked in a basic report, dashboard, or CLI output
- [ ] source code and setup instructions are shared publicly

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- supported platform list actually implemented
- link to code, PR, or public repo/gist
- brief setup and configuration notes
- example syndication runs or logs
- proof of at least one successful outbound post per implemented platform where feasible
- notes on how attribution and scheduling are handled

## Non-Goals
- claiming both this unified bounty and the overlapping single-platform bounties for the same implementation
- one-off manual reposting without a reusable system
- unsupported platform placeholders with no working adapter
- engagement dashboards that exist only as mockups
- blasting all posts at once without platform-aware timing or controls

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for the unified syndication system only. If you are building a single-platform bot, use the narrower bounty for that platform instead. Keep implementation proof, platform list, and payout discussion on this issue.

## Notes
This bounty overlaps with platform-specific bounties such as YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram. The unified system should be treated as a separate higher-level deliverable, not a way to double-claim the same work.

items.title: Video player controls not keyboard accessible
items.body:
## Accessibility Issue: Video player controls not keyboard accessible

**URL**: https://bottube.ai
**Issue type**: Keyboard navigation
**WCAG criterion**: 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A)

### Description
The video player controls (play/pause, volume, fullscreen, seek bar) cannot be operated using keyboard alone. Tab focus does not reach these controls, and there are no keyboard shortcuts documented.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Visit a video page on https://bottube.ai
2. Use Tab key to navigate
3. Attempt to control video playback with keyboard only
4. Observe: controls are not reachable or operable via keyboard

### Expected behavior
All video controls should be focusable and operable via keyboard (Tab, Enter, Space, arrow keys).

### Impact
Users who cannot use a mouse (motor disabilities) cannot control video playback.

**Claiming 1 RTC bounty from**: https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties/issues/1618
**RTC wallet**: RTC1c2d55419fbc69b7b1279130565b8994f3a04b9d

items.title: [Dashboard Request] Elasticsearch Monitoring Dashboard
items.body:
## 📝 Dashboard Request Template

### 1. Dashboard Name

Elasticsearch Monitoring Dashboard

---

### 2. Suggested Dashboard Sections and Panels - Tweak based on relevancy and available metrics

#### Section: Node Metrics
- **Panel**: Node Disk Usage - Displays the disk usage for each Elasticsearch node.
- **Panel**: Node Disk Free - Shows the free disk space for each Elasticsearch node.
- **Panel**: Node Disk Available - Displays the available disk space for each node.
- **Panel**: Node CPU Usage - Displays the CPU usage of each node.
- **Panel**: Node JVM Memory Usage - Displays JVM memory usage for the node.
- **Panel**: Node Heap Memory Usage - Shows the heap memory usage of each node.

#### Section: Cluster Health Metrics
- **Panel**: Cluster Health Status - Shows the overall health of the Elasticsearch cluster.
- **Panel**: Cluster Active Shards - Displays the number of active shards in the cluster.
- **Panel**: Cluster Pending Tasks - Shows the number of pending tasks for the cluster.
- **Panel**: Cluster Unassigned Shards - Displays the number of unassigned shards.

#### Section: Index Metrics
- **Panel**: Index Document Count - Displays the total document count across all indices.
- **Panel**: Index Disk Usage - Shows the total disk usage of indices.
- **Panel**: Index Search Query Count - Displays the total number of search queries on the index.
- **Panel**: Index Search Query Time - Shows the time spent on search queries.

#### Section: Query and Request Metrics
- **Panel**: Search Request Rate - Displays the rate of search requests.
- **Panel**: Query Latency - Shows the latency of search queries.

#### Section: Cache Metrics
- **Panel**: Cache Evictions - Shows the number of cache evictions.
- **Panel**: Cache Hit Count - Displays the number of cache hits.
- **Panel**: Cache Miss Count - Displays the number of cache misses.

---

### 3. Expected Variables

- `deployment.environment`
- `elasticsearch.node.name`
- `elasticsearch.cluster.name`

---

### 4. Additional Comments or Requirements

- This dashboard should focus on both cluster-wide and node-specific health metrics for Elasticsearch, with a particular emphasis on disk, memory, and request latency metrics.

---

### 📋 Notes

Please review the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/SigNoz/dashboards/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on dashboard structure, naming conventions, and how to submit a pull request.

Use Elasticsearch [otel receiver](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/tree/main/receiver/elasticsearchreceiver) and here's [list of available metrics](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/blob/main/receiver/elasticsearchreceiver/metadata.yaml) from the receiver

items.title: [Dashboard Request] AWS MSK Cluster
items.body:
## Dashboard Name

AWS MSK Cluster Monitoring Dashboard

---

## Expected Dashboard Sections and Panels 

### Section: Broker Metrics

- **Panel**: Broker CPU Usage - Displays the CPU utilization for each broker in the MSK cluster.
- **Panel**: Broker Memory Usage - Shows memory consumption by each MSK broker.
- **Panel**: Network Traffic In/Out - Displays the network traffic in and out for each MSK broker.
- **Panel**: Disk Usage - Displays the disk space used by each MSK broker.
- **Panel**: Broker Disk Throughput - Shows the read/write throughput on the disk for each MSK broker.

### Section: Topic Metrics

- **Panel**: Messages In Per Second - Displays the number of messages sent to each topic per second.
- **Panel**: Messages Out Per Second - Displays the number of messages consumed from each topic per second.
- **Panel**: Bytes In Per Second - Shows the volume of data produced to each topic per second.
- **Panel**: Bytes Out Per Second - Shows the volume of data consumed from each topic per second.

### Section: Partition Metrics

- **Panel**: Under-Replicated Partitions - Displays the count of under-replicated partitions across the MSK cluster.
- **Panel**: Partition ISR - Shows the count of in-sync replicas for each partition.
- **Panel**: Partition Count - Displays the number of partitions per topic across the cluster.

### Section: Consumer Metrics

- **Panel**: Consumer Lag - Displays the lag for each consumer group in the MSK cluster.
- **Panel**: Consumer Error Rate - Shows the error rate for consumer requests across the MSK cluster.

### Section: AWS Metrics (CloudWatch Integration)

- **Panel**: CPU Credit Usage - Shows the CPU credit usage for MSK brokers (for burstable instances).
- **Panel**: Burst Balance - Displays the CPU burst balance for MSK brokers.
- **Panel**: Network I/O - Shows the network input/output for the MSK brokers as tracked by AWS CloudWatch.

---

## Expected Dashboard Variables

- `deployment.environment`

---

## Additional Comments or Requirements

- This dashboard should provide comprehensive monitoring for AWS MSK clusters
- The dashboard should integrate with AWS CloudWatch metrics for enhanced visibility into instance-level performance (CPU, network, etc.).

---

## 📋 Notes

Please review the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/SigNoz/dashboards/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on dashboard structure, naming conventions, and how to submit a pull request.

Refer to this [grafana dashboard](https://grafana.com/grafana/dashboards/12009-aws-kafka-cluster/) for reference. Also refer to AWS docs: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2023/04/apache-kafka-aws-distro-opentelemetry/

items.title: [Dashboard] Istio based on prometheus
items.body:
<!-- Use this template to request a new dashboard for the SigNoz Dashboards repository. Providing detailed information will help us understand your needs better and speed up the dashboard creation process. -->

## Dashboard Name

**Istio Monitoring Dashboard**

## Expected Dashboard Sections and Panels

(Can be tweaked (add or remove panels/sections) according to available metrics)

### General Overview

<!-- Brief description of what this section should display (e.g., "High-level metrics providing an overview of Istio's health and performance"). -->

This section provides a high-level overview of the Istio service mesh's health and performance metrics, enabling quick assessment of the overall system state.

#### Panels

- **Total Requests**
  - **Description:** Displays the total number of requests processed by the service mesh.
  
- **Request Rate**
  - **Description:** Shows the rate of incoming and outgoing requests per second.
  
- **Average Latency**
  - **Description:** Illustrates the average latency of requests within the mesh.
  
- **Error Rate**
  - **Description:** Displays the percentage of failed requests compared to total requests.

### Traffic Management

<!-- Brief description of what this section should display (e.g., "Metrics related to traffic routing, load balancing, and service discovery within Istio"). -->

This section focuses on traffic management metrics, providing insights into how traffic is routed, load-balanced, and managed across services.

#### Panels

- **Request Distribution**
  - **Description:** Shows the distribution of requests across different services and versions.
  
- **Load Balancing Efficiency**
  - **Description:** Displays metrics on load balancing effectiveness, such as request distribution fairness.
  
- **Circuit Breaker Events**
  - **Description:** Monitors the number of circuit breaker events triggered, indicating potential service issues.
  
- **Retries and Timeouts**
  - **Description:** Illustrates the number of retry attempts and timeout occurrences in request handling.

### Performance Metrics

<!-- Brief description of what this section should display (e.g., "Metrics related to the performance and responsiveness of services within Istio"). -->

This section provides detailed performance metrics to monitor the responsiveness and efficiency of services managed by Istio.

#### Panels

- **Request Latency Percentiles**
  - **Description:** Displays latency percentiles (e.g., p50, p95, p99) for requests, highlighting performance bottlenecks.
  
- **Throughput**
  - **Description:** Shows the number of requests processed per unit of time, indicating service capacity.
  
- **Service Response Times**
  - **Description:** Illustrates the response times of individual services within the mesh.
  
- **Resource Utilization per Service**
  - **Description:** Monitors CPU and memory usage for each service, identifying resource constraints.

### Error Metrics

<!-- Brief description of what this section should display (e.g., "Metrics related to errors and failures within the Istio service mesh operations"). -->

This section monitors errors and failures within the Istio service mesh, aiding in the troubleshooting and resolution of issues.

#### Panels

- **HTTP Error Rates**
  - **Description:** Displays the rate of HTTP errors (e.g., 4xx, 5xx) across services.
  
- **gRPC Error Rates**
  - **Description:** Shows the rate of gRPC errors encountered in service communications.
  
- **Failed Requests by Service**
  - **Description:** Monitors the number of failed requests attributed to each service.
  
- **TLS Handshake Failures**
  - **Description:** Illustrates the number of TLS handshake failures, indicating potential security or configuration issues.

### Resource Usage

<!-- Brief description of what this section should display (e.g., "Metrics related to the resource consumption of Istio components"). -->

This section provides insights into the resource consumption of Istio components, ensuring efficient operation within the Kubernetes cluster.

#### Panels

- **CPU Usage**
  - **Description:** Displays CPU usage for Istio control plane and data plane components.
  
- **Memory Usage**
  - **Description:** Shows memory consumption of Istio components, helping identify potential memory leaks or inefficiencies.
  
- **Pod Restarts**
  - **Description:** Monitors the number of restarts for Istio pods, indicating stability issues.
  
- **Disk I/O**
  - **Description:** Illustrates disk input/output metrics for Istio components, highlighting storage performance.

### Control Plane Metrics

<!-- Brief description of what this section should display (e.g., "Metrics related to the Istio control plane operations"). -->

This section tracks metrics specific to the Istio control plane, ensuring the configuration and management layers are functioning correctly.

#### Panels

- **Pilot Configuration Syncs**
  - **Description:** Displays the number of configuration synchronizations performed by Pilot.
  
- **Mixer Requests**
  - **Description:** Shows the number of requests handled by Mixer for policy and telemetry.
  
- **Galley Operations**
  - **Description:** Monitors the operations and performance of Galley, Istio's configuration validation component.
  
- **Citadel Certificate Issuance**
  - **Description:** Illustrates the number of certificates issued by Citadel for mutual TLS.

### Data Plane Metrics

<!-- Brief description of what this section should display (e.g., "Metrics related to the Istio data plane operations"). -->

This section provides metrics related to the data plane, focusing on the performance and reliability of sidecar proxies handling service traffic.

#### Panels

- **Envoy Proxy Metrics**
  - **Description:** Displays key metrics from Envoy proxies, such as active connections and request rates.
  
- **Inbound and Outbound Traffic**
  - **Description:** Shows the volume of inbound and outbound traffic handled by data plane proxies.
  
- **Proxy CPU and Memory Usage**
  - **Description:** Monitors resource consumption of Envoy proxies to identify performance issues.
  
- **Connection Errors**
  - **Description:** Illustrates the number of connection errors encountered by data plane proxies.

### Security Metrics

<!-- Brief description of what this section should display (e.g., "Metrics related to security features and policies enforced by Istio"). -->

This section tracks security-related metrics, ensuring that Istio's security features are effectively protecting the service mesh.

#### Panels

- **Mutual TLS Usage**
  - **Description:** Displays the number of connections secured with mutual TLS.
  
- **Authorization Policy Enforcement**
  - **Description:** Shows metrics related to the enforcement of authorization policies, including allowed and denied requests.
  
- **Certificate Expirations**
  - **Description:** Monitors the expiration status of certificates managed by Istio, ensuring timely renewals.
  
- **Security Policy Violations**
  - **Description:** Illustrates the number of security policy violations detected within the mesh.

## Expected Dashboard Variables

- `namespace` – Filter metrics based on the Kubernetes namespace where Istio is deployed.
- `deployment.environment` - Environment of application (configured at Otel agent level)
- `service.name` – Select specific services within the mesh to filter metrics.
- `cluster` – For multi-cluster setups, filter metrics based on the Kubernetes cluster.

## References or Screenshots

- [Istio Prometheus Metrics Documentation](https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/metrics/)
- [Istio Observability Documentation](https://istio.io/latest/docs/concepts/observability/)
- [Sample Istio Dashboard Screenshot](https://grafana.com/grafana/dashboards/7645-istio-control-plane-dashboard/)

## 📋 Notes

Please review the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/SigNoz/dashboards/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on dashboard structure, naming conventions, and how to submit a pull request.

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1-003: REST API — Bounty CRUD Endpoints
items.body:
## Bounty: REST API — Bounty CRUD Endpoints

**Tier:** 1 (Open Race) | **Reward:** 250 $FNDRY | **Timeout:** 72h

---

### Description

Build the core REST API for managing bounties — create, read, update, list with filtering.

### Requirements

- FastAPI (Python) or Express (TypeScript)
- Endpoints:
  - `POST /api/bounties` — create bounty (title, description, tier, reward, skills, deadline)
  - `GET /api/bounties` — list with filters (tier, status, skills) and pagination
  - `GET /api/bounties/:id` — get single bounty
  - `PATCH /api/bounties/:id` — update status (open → in_progress → completed → paid)
  - `POST /api/bounties/:id/submit` — submit a PR URL as a solution
- Input validation + error handling
- PostgreSQL schema (migrations included)
- Docker compose for local dev (API + Postgres)
- OpenAPI/Swagger docs auto-generated

### Deliverables

- `backend/` directory with full API
- `backend/schema.sql` or Alembic migrations
- `docker-compose.yml`
- README with setup instructions
- Basic tests

### How to Submit

Fork → implement in `backend/` → open PR against `main`

**Open race — first valid PR wins 250 $FNDRY**

`$FNDRY: C2TvY8E8B75EF2UP8cTpTp3EDUjTgjWmpaGnT74VBAGS`

items.title: [BOUNTY] Liquidity Provider Incentive Program — Earn 500 RTC/month for wRTC/SOL LP (1,500 RTC Pilot)
items.body:
## Liquidity Provider Incentive Program

**Budget**: 500 RTC/month (3-month pilot, 1,500 RTC total from community fund)
**Pool**: wRTC/SOL on Raydium  
**Pool ID**: `8CF2Q8nSCxRacDShbtF86XTSrYjueBMKmfdR3MLdnYzb`

### The Problem

wRTC launched on Raydium with ~$765 liquidity. That's too thin for any serious trader — a $50 buy causes ~7% slippage. We need deeper liquidity to make wRTC actually tradeable.

### How It Works

1. **Add liquidity** to the wRTC/SOL pool on Raydium
2. **Hold your LP position** for at least 30 days
3. At month-end, share your LP token balance (screenshot + wallet address)
4. **Rewards distributed** proportional to your share of total external LP

### Reward Tiers

| LP Value (USD) | Monthly Reward |
|----------------|----------------|
| $50–$499 | Pro-rata share of 500 RTC pool |
| $500–$999 | Pro-rata share + 10% bonus |
| $1,000+ | Pro-rata share + 25% bonus |

### Example

If the 500 RTC monthly pool has 3 LPs:
- Alice: $200 LP → gets ~100 RTC
- Bob: $500 LP → gets ~275 RTC (pro-rata + 10% bonus)
- Carol: $100 LP → gets ~50 RTC

### Proof Required

1. Wallet address that holds LP tokens
2. Screenshot of Raydium LP position showing wRTC/SOL pool
3. On-chain TX of original liquidity add (verifiable on Solscan)
4. LP must be held for full 30-day period (no add/remove cycling)

### Links

| Resource | URL |
|----------|-----|
| **Swap wRTC** | https://raydium.io/swap/?inputMint=sol&outputMint=12TAdKXxcGf6oCv4rqDz2NkgxjyHq6HQKoxKZYGf5i4X |
| **Add Liquidity** | https://raydium.io/liquidity/increase/?mode=add&pool_id=8CF2Q8nSCxRacDShbtF86XTSrYjueBMKmfdR3MLdnYzb |
| **DexScreener** | https://dexscreener.com/solana/8CF2Q8nSCxRacDShbtF86XTSrYjueBMKmfdR3MLdnYzb |
| **Solscan (Token)** | https://solscan.io/token/12TAdKXxcGf6oCv4rqDz2NkgxjyHq6HQKoxKZYGf5i4X |
| **Bridge** | https://bottube.ai/bridge |

### Token Info

- **Mint**: `12TAdKXxcGf6oCv4rqDz2NkgxjyHq6HQKoxKZYGf5i4X`
- **Total Supply**: 8,300,000 wRTC (fixed)
- **Mint Authority**: REVOKED (no more can ever be minted)
- **Metadata**: IMMUTABLE
- **Reference Rate**: 1 RTC = $0.10 USD

### Rules

- Minimum LP position: $50 equivalent
- No wash trading — LP add/remove cycling within 30 days disqualifies
- One claim per wallet per month
- 30-day clawback if LP removed early
- Program runs for 3 months (renewable based on results)

### How to Claim

Comment on this issue at month-end with:
1. Your wallet address
2. Screenshot of your LP position
3. TX hash of your liquidity add

Rewards paid in RTC to your RustChain wallet within 48 hours of verification.

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1-005: GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline
items.body:
## Bounty: GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline

**Tier:** 1 (Open Race) | **Reward:** 100 $FNDRY | **Timeout:** 72h

---

### Description

Set up the CI/CD pipeline for the SolFoundry monorepo — linting, testing, building, and deploying.

### Requirements

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — runs on every PR:
  - Lint (ESLint for TS, Ruff for Python, Clippy for Rust)
  - Type check (tsc --noEmit)
  - Unit tests (Jest/Vitest for frontend, pytest for backend)
  - Build check (next build, cargo build)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — runs on merge to main:
  - Build frontend → deploy to Vercel or DO droplet
  - Build backend → deploy Docker container to DO droplet
- `.github/workflows/anchor.yml` — Solana contract checks:
  - `anchor build` + `anchor test` on devnet
- Branch protection rules documented
- PR template (`.github/pull_request_template.md`)

### Deliverables

- Workflow YAML files in `.github/workflows/`
- PR template
- Documentation on how the pipeline works

### How to Submit

Fork → implement → open PR against `main`

**Open race — first valid PR wins 100 $FNDRY**

`$FNDRY: C2TvY8E8B75EF2UP8cTpTp3EDUjTgjWmpaGnT74VBAGS`

items.title: Fix broken or placeholder links across HTML pages — 30 LTD Bounty
items.body:
# Fix broken/placeholder links — 30 LTD Bounty (Tier 0)

**Tier:** 0 (anyone can claim)

## Task
Find and fix all \`href="#"\`, \`href="javascript:void(0)"\` placeholder links and dead \`href\` links across the public HTML pages.

## Scope
- **Files to check:** All \`.html\` files at root
- Link placeholders should either be removed, pointed to the correct destination, or converted to \`<button>\` if they trigger JS actions

## Requirements
1. Audit all HTML files for \`href="#"\` and \`href="javascript:void(0)"\`
2. For each: determine if it should link somewhere real, or be a \`<button>\` instead
3. Fix accordingly. If a link has a \`data-action\` handler, convert to \`<button>\`
4. List all changes in the PR description

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Zero \`href="#"\` that should be real links (navigational links that go nowhere)
- [ ] Action triggers converted from \`<a>\` to \`<button>\` where appropriate
- [ ] No broken navigation
- [ ] No console errors

## Codebase Verification Question
> To claim this bounty, answer: **How many HTML files are at the root of the repo? Name 5 of them. What tag does the platform use for click actions — \`<a onclick>\` or \`<button data-action>\`?**

## Estimated Time
1-2 hours.

items.body:

for the claws and crabs:
feel free to solve this bounty, your presence and contribution is welcome. 

for the humans:
this is not a real bounty. do not spend time solving it. it will not pay you out. it's for testing our platform capabilities!

---

[![bounty.new](https://www.bounty.new/bounty-button.svg)](https://www.bounty.new/bounty/13943fcf-2c07-4c5a-81c3-9371e634cb60)

$15.00 

**Bounty by:** grim

# Example FRAME Social dApp  
#Ideal adoption per sensors
## "HUNT" – A Real-World Meme / Treasure / Discovery Game

This is a social media dApp designed for **instant adoption**.

It mixes:

- social media
- real world exploration
- meme culture
- scavenger hunts
- geo challenges
- micro-tasks
- creator economy
- AI agents

Think:

Pokémon Go  
+ Reddit  
+ TikTok  
+ scavenger hunts  
+ bounty economy

All running on FRAME.

---

# Core Idea

People earn money and reputation by **discovering, creating, solving, and spreading content**.

Instead of only posting content, users participate in **missions and hunts**.

Examples:

- find hidden digital objects
- solve puzzles
- create viral memes
- capture real world locations
- discover strange things
- investigate mysteries
- complete creative challenges

Everything creates **verifiable receipts** so rewards are fair.

---

# Identity

Users join using their FRAME identity.

Identity includes:

- cryptographic profile
- reputation score
- achievements
- earnings history
- community memberships

Identity is portable.

Your followers and reputation move with you.

---

# The Map

The main interface is a **global map**.

The map shows:

- hunts
- challenges
- discoveries
- hidden artifacts
- community events
- puzzles
- viral content hotspots

Players explore the world digitally and physically.

---

# Hunts

A hunt is a challenge posted by anyone.

Example hunts:

- find a specific location
- photograph a rare object
- record a strange sound
- solve a puzzle
- decode a message
- locate a hidden QR artifact
- recreate a meme
- capture a unique event

Each hunt includes:

- reward
- rules
- verification logic

The first person to complete the hunt gets paid.

---

# Meme Economy

Memes become **competitive challenges**.

Example:

Someone posts:

"Create the funniest version of this meme"

Community votes.

Winner receives rewards.

Because posts are signed, the original creator is always known.

---

# Viral Challenge System

Users can start challenges like:

- funniest video
- best street photo
- weirdest discovery
- most creative remix

People participate and vote.

Rewards come from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community funding

---

# Discovery Mode

Users can post **discoveries**.

Examples:

- abandoned places
- weird architecture
- hidden murals
- strange natural phenomena
- cool restaurants
- secret viewpoints
- urban legends

Discoveries become permanent map objects.

People earn rewards if the discovery becomes popular.

---

# Bounties

Anyone can create a bounty.

Example:

Find this location  
Solve this puzzle  
Capture this photo  
Investigate this mystery

Users race to complete it.

The bounty creator deposits funds into a contract.

The winner receives payment automatically.

---

# AI Generated Hunts

Agents generate hunts automatically.

Examples:

- locate unique graffiti
- photograph a rare plant
- capture unusual cloud formations
- document historic buildings

Agents analyze submissions and verify authenticity.

---

# Verification

FRAME allows hunts to be verified using:

- GPS location
- timestamp
- image analysis
- sensor data
- community verification
- agent validation

This prevents cheating.

---

# Local Community Hunts

Cities can host events.

Example:

City tourism hunt.

Participants must:

- visit landmarks
- photograph locations
- answer trivia questions
- collect digital artifacts

Players earn rewards and achievements.

---

# Mystery Hunts

Communities can build complex puzzles.

Example:

ARG style mystery.

Players decode clues across:

- posts
- locations
- cryptography puzzles
- hidden objects

Winning teams receive large rewards.

---

# Creator Economy

Creators earn money by creating:

- hunts
- puzzles
- games
- community events
- meme challenges

Popular hunts generate revenue.

---

# Sponsor Hunts

Businesses can sponsor hunts.

Example:

Coffee shop hunt.

Players must:

- visit store
- photograph special item
- answer trivia

Players receive rewards.

Businesses gain foot traffic.

---

# Reputation

Players build reputation through:

- discoveries
- successful hunts
- puzzle solving
- content creation
- community votes

High reputation unlocks larger hunts.

---

# Global Leaderboards

Leaderboards include:

- top explorers
- top creators
- top puzzle solvers
- top meme creators
- top communities

---

# Digital Artifacts

Players can collect artifacts.

Examples:

- rare meme templates
- historical discoveries
- puzzle trophies
- city badges

Artifacts can be traded.

---

# AR Integration

Augmented reality can display:

- hidden objects
- digital creatures
- puzzle clues
- collectible artifacts

Players explore cities like a game.

---

# Real World Missions

Large missions could include:

- documenting environmental changes
- mapping hiking trails
- identifying plants
- reporting infrastructure issues

Users earn money for useful discoveries.

---

# Community Worlds

Communities can create themed worlds.

Examples:

- paranormal hunters
- abandoned place explorers
- architecture photography
- meme creators
- puzzle solvers

Each world has its own hunts and rewards.

---

# Money Flow

Money enters the system from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community pools
- creators
- brands
- city governments

Players earn rewards by participating.

---

# Why People Would Instantly Use It

It solves multiple things people already love:

People love:

- social media
- viral challenges
- scavenger hunts
- puzzles
- memes
- competition
- earning money online

This combines them into one platform.

---

# Why FRAME Makes It Better

FRAME enables:

- verifiable rewards
- identity ownership
- fair payouts
- portable reputation
- no platform lock-in
- decentralized moderation

Users keep their audience and earnings.

---

# Long Term Evolution

Over time the system becomes:

- global discovery network
- collaborative knowledge system
- real world exploration game
- meme economy
- bounty network

People earn money by **being curious, creative, and observant**.

---

# End Vision

The app turns the real world into a **giant interactive game**.

People explore cities.

Solve puzzles.

Create memes.

Document strange discoveries.

Earn money.

And build communities around curiosity and creativity.
